description: This schematic diagram illustrates the complete thermomechanical processing route for Fe–20Mn–9Al-1.5C–2Ni–3Cr low-density steel, starting from ingot homogenization at 1200°C for 2 h, followed by hot-rolling to 6 mm thickness at 1050–950°C and subsequent air cooling. The diagram bifurcates into two distinct heat treatment strategies: single-stage treatments (labeled S1000, S1050, S1100) involving austenitization at 1000°C, 1050°C, or 1100°C for 3 min followed by quenching to room temperature; and a multi-stage heat treatment (MHT) comprising three sequential steps—first stage at 1000°C for 3 min, an intermediate stage at 750°C for 15 min, and a final stage at 1050°C for 3 min, all with quenching. The visual layout emphasizes the critical role of the intermediate 750°C treatment in precipitating grain boundary phases that serve as twin nucleation sites, contrasting with the direct quenching approaches of single-stage variants. This figure establishes the experimental framework for investigating grain boundary engineering effects on impact toughness while maintaining high strength and ductility.
